source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The three-bus power system shown in the figure has one alternator connected to bus 2, which supplies 200 MW and 40 MVAR power. Bus 3 is an infinite bus having a voltage of magnitude \( |V_3| = 1.0 \text{ p.u.} \) and an angle of \(-15^\circ\). A variable current source, \( |I|\angle\phi \) is connected at bus 1 and controlled such that the magnitude of the bus 1 voltage is maintained at 1.05 p.u. and the phase angle of the source current, \(\phi = \theta_1 \pm \frac{\pi}{2}\), where \(\theta_1\) is the phase angle of the bus 1 voltage. The three buses can be categorized for load flow analysis as:
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: Bus 1 \( P - |V| \) bus, Bus 2 \( P - Q \) bus, Bus 3 Slack bus

choice_map.A: Bus 1 Slack bus, Bus 2 \( P - |V| \) bus, Bus 3 \( P - Q \) bus
choice_map.B: Bus 1 \( P - |V| \) bus, Bus 2 \( P - |V| \) bus, Bus 3 Slack bus
choice_map.C: Bus 1 \( P - Q \) bus, Bus 2 \( P - Q \) bus, Bus 3 Slack bus
choice_map.D: Bus 1 \( P - |V| \) bus, Bus 2 \( P - Q \) bus, Bus 3 Slack bus

issues: metadata.extraction_notes indicate the source image field contained an in-memory PIL image object placeholder rather than a path/URL.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'shown in the figure'
the bus-device configuration is tied to bus indices in the depicted three-bus system, although substantial device information is also stated in text
multi_step: the task requires classifying all three buses, not extracting a single stated fact
bus 1 classification depends on combining voltage-magnitude control with the current-angle constraint phi = theta1 ± pi/2
bus 2 and bus 3 must be identified from different operating descriptions (alternator specified by P and Q; infinite bus with fixed magnitude and angle)
there are 4 answer choices, indicating a structured reasoning/comparison task rather than direct lookup
verifiability: answer is a discrete bus-type assignment and matches one of the provided multiple-choice options
answer_text is explicit: 'Bus 1 P-|V| bus, Bus 2 P-Q bus, Bus 3 Slack bus'
the bus classifications can be checked against standard load-flow bus definitions

open_ended_problem_variants.title: Bus 2 Classification
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text: In the given three-bus power system, bus 2 has one alternator connected to it supplying 200 MW and 40 MVAR power. For load flow analysis, how should bus 2 be categorized?

open_ended_problem_variants.title: Bus 3 Classification
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text: In the given three-bus power system, bus 3 is an infinite bus with voltage magnitude \(|V_3| = 1.0\ \text{p.u.}\) and angle \(-15^\circ\). For load flow analysis, how should bus 3 be categorized?
